(8 marks) 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： 得分标准： Sales nominal 斤 二 1 VC nominal 1 FC nominal —C __ 1 Tax liability 1 TAD 1 TAD benefits 1 Tax timing 1 PVs and nominal NPV 1 Total 8 知识点标签：Investment appraisal-Allowing for inflation and taxation in DCF-Real-terms and nominal-terms approaches 难易程度：中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路解析，可供同学参考： 题目要求计算名义净现值。先找出项目第一年到第四年的相关现金流，然后使用对应的通胀 率计算出名义的现金流，统计出名义净现金流，使用名义资本成本折现，算出名义净现金流 的现值之和。最后，名义净现金流的现值之和减去初始投资金额等于项目的名义净现值。 第一步，确定相关现金流： initial investment,sales revenue,variable cost,fixed cost,taxation。 注意，题目没有特别说明的情况下，题目给出的fixed cost 默认为incremental fixed cost,是 相关现金流。 第二步，使用对应的通胀率计算出名义的现金流。 Selling price 的通胀率是5%,1 - 4年的 nominal selling price per unit 依次为125*1.05¹,130*1.05²,140*1.05³,120*1.054。每年的 nominal selling price per unit 乘以这 一 年的 sales volume, 可以算出每年的nominal sales revenue 。同 理 ，variable cost 的通胀率是3 . 5%,1-4年的nominal variable cost per unit 依次 为71*1.035¹,71*1.035²,71*1.035³,71*1.035⁴。每年的 nominal variable cost 乘以这一年 的 sales volume,可以算出每年的nominal variable cost 。Fixed cost 不会受到销售数量的影响， fixed cost 的通胀率为6%,1-4年的fixed cost 分别为3000*1.06¹,3100*1.06²,3200*1.06³, 3000*1.06⁴。 第三步，计算 tax 。税率为26%,在产生营运现金流的同一年交税。先算出1-4年的产生的 营运现金流 operating cash flow before tax=sales revenue-variable cost-fixed cost, 每一年的 税等于每年operating cash flow*tax rate(26%), 交税时间为1-4年。 第四步，计算TAD benefit。先算出每一年的税务允许的折旧 TAD 。题目指出使用递减折旧 法，每年的折旧为资产期初余额的25%。1-3年的 TAD 依次为20000*25%=5000, (20000-5000)*25%=3750,(20000-5000-3750)*25%=2813,第4年的TAD 等于资产的期初余 额减去资产的残值，这道题目中，资产残值为0,TAD=(20000-5000-3750-2813)-0=8437。 TAD benefit=每 年 的TAD* 税率，如第一年，TAD benefit=5000*26%=1300, 如此类推。 第五步，计算名义净现值NPV 。 计算出每年税后名义现金流净值，使用名义税后资本成本 12%作为折现率折现，方法是查表 present value table, 可以找出每年的discount factor(DF), 每年税后名义现金流净值乘以DF 等于这一年的名义净现金流的现值。然后算出1-4年的税 后名义现金流的现值之和。最后，使用名义净现金流的现值之和减去初始投资金额算出项目 的名义净现值。 (a)(ii)Calculate the real net present value of Pinks Co's investment project and comment on your findings. (4 marks) 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： 得分标准： Real CF before tax 1 Tax treatment 1 PVs and real NPV 1 Comment 1 Total 4 知识点标签：Investment appraisal-Allowing for inflation and taxation in DCF-Real-terms and nominal-terms approaches 难易程度：高 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路解析，可供同学参考： 题目要求计算实际净现值。先对税前名义现金流nominal cash flow before tax 去通胀，算 出 real cash flow before tax。然后减去税，加上TAD benefit, 算出每年的实际净现金流， 使用实际资本成本折现，求出每年的实际净现金流的现值之和。最后，实际净现金流的 现值之和减去初始投资金额等于项目的实际净现值。实际净现值大于0,项目可接受。 实际净现值小于0,项目不可接受。 第一步，(i) 小问中已经找出税前名义现金流 nominal cash flow before tax, 使用平均通胀 率3.7%去通胀，计算出每年的税前实际现金流real cash flow before tax 。1-4年的具体算 法分别为，14148*1.037-¹=13643,24097*1.037-²=22408,39948*1.037-³=35823, 10379*1.037⁴=8975。 第二步，算出 tax 以及TAD benefit。每 一 年的Tax=real cash flow before tax*tax rate (26%) 。Initial investment 和资产残值不受通胀影响，因此TAD benefit 和(i) 一 样。 第三步，计算实际净现值NPV 。计算出每年税后实际现金流净值，使用实际税后资本成本 8%作为折现率折现，方法是查表present value table, 可以找出每年的discount factor(DF), 每年税后实际现金流净值乘以DF 等于这一年的实际净现金流的现值。然后算出1-4年的税 后实际现金流的现值之和。最后，使用实际净现金流的现值之和减去初始投资金额算出项目 的实际净现值。 (b)Discuss FOUR ways to encourage managers to achieve stakeholder objectives.(8 marks) (20 marks) 题目类型：Word 官方答案： 得分标准： First way 2 Second way 2 Third way 2 Fourth way 2 Total 8 知识点标签：Financial management function-Stakeholders and impact on corporate objectives- Role of management in meeting stakeholder objectives 难易程度：高 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题要求讨论4种方式鼓励管理者实现利益相关者的目标。股东是企业的最重要的利益相关 者，这道题需要我们讨论的是如何实现股东财富最大化。企业的所有者是股东，企业的管理 者是高管。高管是股东的代理，高管在做决策时有可能不是把股东的利益放在首位，而是把 个人利益放在首位。这就是代理问题。减少代理问题的发生，有助于实现股东财富最大化。 因此这道题的讨论围绕着如何减少代理问题的发生。常用的做法有，推出股票期权计划，制 定绩效奖金计划，使用最高标准的公司治理，证券市场对上市公司的监管，对高管的决策和 管理进行监督。讨论时，选出4种方式。阐述每一种方式具体内容以及优缺点。 第1种方式：股票期权计划。股票期权计划指的是给予高管在未来某一天有权以特定的价格 购买特定数量的股票。假如高管做出的决策有利于股价长期增长，无论是高管还是股东，都 可以因此获益。因此股票期权计划有助于实现高管和股东的目标的一致性。但是股票期权计 划有可能使公司产生比较大的成本，所以在制定时要谨慎。 第2种方式：绩效奖金计划。给管理者制定业绩目标，当管理者实现目标时，给予管理者 奖励。这就是绩效奖金计划。绩效奖金计划有助于实现股东财富最大化。给管理者制定的业 绩目标，通常是每股收益。当然也有可能给管理者制定一个减少环境污染的目标，比如说减 少二氧化碳的排量。在制定业绩目标时要非常的谨慎，一定要保证这个目标有助于实现股东 财富最大化。要注意的是，有一些业绩目标，可能会引发代理问题。 第3种方式：使用最高标准的公司治理。高标准的公司治理指的是建立薪酬委员会，充分的 进行风险评估和风险管理，对企业财务数据进行审计，建立完善的内控系统，明确执行董事 长的责任，建立完善的追责体系。非执行董事长在公司治理中扮演着重要的角色。显然高标 准的公司治理有助于实现股东财富最大化。 第4种方式：证券市场对上市公司的监管。这是针对上市公司的管理。为了保证证券市场的 公平性和有效性，证监会会制定一系列的监管规则。监管规则包括要求上市公司披露价格敏 感的信息，以减少股东和管理者之间的信息不对称。这样一来，股东能更加了解企业的情况。 有利于股东做出正确的投资决策。 第5种方式：对高管的决策和管理进行监督。常见的一种监督方式是对高管编制的财务报表 进行审计。审计有助于保证提供给投资者的财务信息是真实和公允的。有利于股东做出正确 的投资决策。 1 、2020MJ Q1 Pumice 本题核心知识点： Management of inventories,accounts receivable,accounts payable and cash& Determining working capital needs and funding strategies (a)(i)Prepare a forecast statement of financial position for Pumice co;and (6 marks) (ii)Calculates the effect of the proposed expansion on the working capital ratios listed by the finance directors (4 marks) 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： (a)(i)Forecast statement of financial position for Pumice Co Assets $'000 Non-current assets 60,018 Current assets Inventory 4,394 Trade receivables 15,979 Cash 700 21,073 Total assets 81,091 Equity and liabilities $'000 Equity 6,000 Reserves 40,818 Total equity 46,818 Non-current liabilities 26,000 Current liabilities Trade payables 5,273 Overdraft 3,000 8.273 Total equity and liabilities 81,091 Workings Non-current assets=54,070x1.11=$60,017,700 Revenue =80,768x1.187=$95,871,616 Cost of sales=95,871,616x0.33=$31,637,633 Inventory =31,637,633x50/360=$4,394,116 Trade receivables =95,871,616x60/360=$15,978,603 Reserves=34,000+6,818=$40,818,000 Non-current liabilities=18,000+8,000=$26,000,000 Trade payables=31,637,633x60/360=$5,272,939 (a)(ii)Trade payables payment period Currently this is(9,690/27,700)x360=126 days The company is targeting 60 days,a reduction of(126-60)=66 days 高顿教育 This looks to be ambitious. Current ratio Currently this is 17,120/13,190=1.30 times After the expansion this becomes 21,073/8,273= 2.55 times This suggests the company is looking to increase solvency. Revenue/net working capital Net working capital currently=4,000+12,320-9,690=$6,630,000 Revised net working capital=4,394+15,979-5,273=$15,100,000 Revenue/NWC currently=80,768/6,630=12.18 times Revised revenue/NWC=95,872/15,100=6.35 times There would be a substantial increase in the capital supporting revenue. 得分标准： (a) (i)Non-current assets 0.5 (i)Revenue 0.5 (i)Cost of sales 0.5 (i)Inventory 1 (i)Trade receivables 1 (i)Trade payables 1 (i)Reserves 0.5 (i)Non-current liabilities 0.5 (i)Overdraf/cash 0.5 (ii)Trade payables period 1 (ii)Current ratio 1 (ii)Revenue/Net working capital 1 (ii)Changes 1 知识点标签：Working capital management---Management of inventories,accounts receivable, accounts payable and cash 难易程度：高 解析： (a)问要求预测出扩张后的资产负债表，已经分别算出对题目中要求的三个working capital ratio 的影响。 第一小题的解题逻辑为根据题目给出扩张前的资产负债表以及其变化来一个个算出每个科 目的预测值。

stem_zh: 1. 题目中说到NCA 会增加11%,因此我们可到Forecast NCA=54,070x1.11=$60,017,700, 约等于60,018,000。 2.Credit sales 增加18.7%,可得到Forecast Revenue=80,768x1.187=$95,871,616 。Cost of sales 等于33%的credit sales,可得Forecast Cost of sales=95,871,616x0.33=$31,637,633。
3. 根据上述得到的credit sales和cost ofsales,我们可以利用周转天数公式分别计算出存货、 应收账款和应付账款： Inventory =31,637,633x50/360=$4,394,116 Trade receivables=95,871,616x60/360=$15,978,603 Trade payables=31,637,633x60/360=$5,272,939
4. 根据题意，Cash balance 在扩张后等于700,000。 5.Equity 由于未提到发行股票融资，因此保持不变，依旧为6,000,000 6.Reserves 应该加上新产生的 PAT:6.818m, 因此可得到 Reserves =34,000+6,818= $40,818,000

7. 题目中提到公司会发行$8m 的债券来对扩张计划进行融资，因此NCL 应新增8m, Non-current liabilities =18,000+8,000=$26,000,000 8.Overdraft 从3,500,000降低到3,000,000。 将上述数值计算出后，可直接填入资产负债表中，得到最终的答案。 第二小题的计算逻辑是计算扩张前的三个相关ratio,再与题目给到的扩张后的数据进行对 比。 首先应付账款周转天数，现在为(9,690/27,700)x 360=126 days 公司制定的扩张后的目标为 60 days, 下降了(126-60)=66 days, 下降了超过一半，这是一个过于激进的目标。 其次对于流动比率现在为(17,120/13,190=1.30倍，而扩张后的目标为21,073/8,273=2.55 倍。流动比率增加了很多，代表公司有在努力提高流动性。 最后对于Revenue/net working capital, 现在的Net working capital=4,000+12,320-9,690= $6,630,000,改变后的 net working capital=4,394+15,979-5,273=$15,100,000 因此可以得到现在的Revenue/NWC=80,768/6,630=12.18 times,改变后的 revenue/NWC= 95,872/15,100=6.35 times,公司需要增加非常多的NWC 才有可能实现这个目标。 (b)Discuss the ways in which implementing the proposed changes in working capital represent: (i) Changes in working capital investment policy for Pumice Co;and (5marks ) (ii) Changes in working capital funding policy for Pumice Co. (5 marks) 题目类型：Word 官 方 答 案： (b)(i)Changes in working capital investment policy Working capital investment policy considers the level of current assets used to support revenue generation in relation to different companies. A company adopts an aggressive working capital investment policy relative to another company if it uses a lower level of current assets to support a similar level of revenue generation.Conversely, the second company adopts a conservative working capital investment policy relative to the first company. While there are no companies here with which to compare Pumice Co's working capital investment policy,the effect of implementing the proposed changes in working capital can be measured by the revenue/current assets ratio.This shows that no significant change has occurred as a result of implementing the proposed changes in working capital,as it has only changed from 4.72 times to 4.55 times. Revenue/current assets appears a less sensitive measure of working capital investment policy than revenue/net working capital,which has changed from 12.18 times to 6.35 times as a result of the decreased reliance on short-term working capital funding implicit in the proposed changes in working capital. Workings Revenue/current assets now=80,768/17,120=4.72 times Revised revenue/current assets=95,872/21,073=4.55 times Net working capital currently=4,000+12,320-9,690=$6,630,000 Revised net working capital=4,394+15,979-5,273=$15,100,000 Revenue/NWC currently=80,768/6,630=12.18 times Revised revenue/NWC=95,872/15,100=6.35 times (ii)Changes in working capital funding policy Working capital funding policy can be characterised as conservative,matching or aggressive, depending on the extent to which fluctuating current assets and permanent current assets are financed from short-term or long-term sources. A conservative funding policy will use long-term funds to finance permanent current assets and a proportion of fluctuating current assets.This is a lower-risk policy as long-term funds are less risky than short-term funds from a company perspective,but as long-term funds are more expensive than short-term funds,this policy also decreases profitability. An aggressive funding policy will use short-term funds to finance fluctuating current assets and a proportion of permanent current assets.This is a higher-risk policy as short-term funds are more risky than long-term funds from a company perspective,but as short-term funds are cheaper than long-term funds,this policy also increases profitability A matching funding policy would apply the matching principle in using short-term funds to finance fluctuating current assets and using long-term funds to finance permanent current assets. While there is insufficient information to determine the relative levels of permanent and fluctuating current assets,implementing the proposed changes in working capital shows a substantial movement to using long-term funds rather than shortterm funds.Before the expansion, 77%of current assets are financed from shortterm funds (trade payables plus overdraft).After the expansion,only 39%of current assets would be financed for short-term funds and 61%would be financed from longterm funds.This change is also apparent from the increase in the current ratio from 1.30 times to 2.55 times. The proposed changes in working capital therefore suggest a movement by Pumice Co from an aggressive working capital funding policy to a conservative working capital funding policy. This view is also evidenced by the $4,917,000 decrease in short-term funds relative to the $3,953,000 increase in current assets and the $14,818,000 increase in longterm funds:the company's current reliance on short-term funds has been reversed. Workings Current assets financed by short-term funds: Before expansion=100x(13,190/17,120)=77% After expansion=100x(8,273/21,073)=39% Decrease in short-term funds=13,190-8,273=$4,917,000 Increase in current assets=21,073-17,120=$3,953,000 Increase in long-term funds=(46,818+26,000)-(40,000+18,000)=$14,818,000 得分标准： (b) (i)Aggressive investment policy 1 (i)Conservative investment policy 1 (i)Revenue/Current assets 1 (i)Changes discussion 2 5 ii)Conservative financing policy 1 ii)Aggressive financing policy 1 (ii)Matching financing policy 1 (ii)Changes discussion 2 5 知识点标签：Working capital management---Determining working capital needs and funding strategies 难易程度： 中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题问的是Pumice 的营运资本投资政策和融资政策的变化：首先，需要分别介绍一下营运 资本投资政策和融资政策以及其三种类型： aggressivelconservativelmoderate。 接下来需要结合题意分析 Pumice 现在所属于的类型，以及扩张后的类型。这里需要计算一 些数据来佐证我们的结论。 对于营运资本投资政策来说，我们可以计算revenues/current assets ratio 以 及revenues/NWC ratio。revenues/current assets ratio在扩张前后变化不大，所以我们主要看 revenues/NWC ratio, 其从6.35增加到12. 18,说明公司持有了非常多的长期资本，可能暗示着公司向保守的营运 资本投资政策转变。 对于营运资本融资政策来说，由于题目中没有给到 permanent 和 fluctuating current assets的 相关数据，我们只能直接看current assets 的融资比重。通过计算我们可以发现扩张前，current assets有77%是通过短期融资，而扩张后该比例降低到了39%,说明公司用了更多的长期融 资，代表公司从激进向保守的营运资本融资政策转变。 2、2020 MJQ2 LaForge 本题核心知识点： Business finance (3 marks)(1 marks)(a)For the rights issue,calculate the following: (3 marks) (1 marks) (i)The theoretical ex-rights price per share;and (ii)The value of a right per existing shares 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： (a)(i)TERP Current number of shares=$35m/$0.50=70m Issue price=$2.60x(1-0.3)=$1.82 per share Number of shares to be issued=$25.48m/$1.82=14 million shares TERP=(70mx$2.60+$25.48m)/(70m+14m)=$2.47 per share Alternatively: Ratio of issued shares=14m:70m=0.2,and therefore al for 5issue TERP=($1.82+5x$2.60)/(1+5)=$2.47 per share (a)(ii)Value of a right (VOR) VOR per new share=$2.47-$1.82=$0.65 per new share VOR per existing share=$0.65x14m/70m=$0.13 per existing share Alternately using 1 for 5 ratio=$0.6515=$0.13 per existing share 得分标准： (a) (i)Existing shares 0.5 (i)New shares 0.5 (i)Issue price 1 (i)TERP l 3 (ii)Value of rights per new share 0.5 (ii)Value of rights per existing share 0.5 1 知识点标签：Business finance---Sources of,and raising,business finance---Methods of raising equity finance 难易程度：低
explanation_zh: (a)问要求计算TERP 和 value of right per existing share。 TERP 我们需要计算出发行价格和发行股数，已知发行价格30%折扣，可到Issue price=$2.60 x(1-0.3)=$1.82 per share。 题目中说到需要的融资金额为$25.48m, 而发行价格为$1.82,可以计算出需要发行的股数= $25.48m/$1.82=14 million shares。 因此TERP=(70m x $2.60+$25.48m)/(70m+14m)=$2.47 per share 计算出TERP 后，我们可以算出 VOR per new share=TERP-issue price=$2.47-$1.82=$0.65 per new share 。这是每张新股对应的VOR, 一般情况下我们需要知道配股的比例再计算现 有每股对应的VOR 。 但这道题并没有给到关于配股比例的相关信息。因此我们可以用总新 股数量/现有股票的数量来计算配股比例：14/70=0.2,也就是1 for 5 rights issue,因 此VOR per existing share=0.65/5=0.13。 (b)Assuming that the investment goes ahead,calculate LaForge Co's forecast earnings per share,for the coming year AND the resulting share price,if it finances the investment using each of these alternatives: (i)The rights issue;and (ii)The loan notes (2 marks) (3 marks) 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： (b)(i)Rights issue Forecast PAT=$16.56m+$4.5m x(1-0.2)=$20.16m Forecast EPS=$20.16m/84m=$0.24 per share Forecast share price=$0.24x11=$2.64 per share (b)(ii)Loan notes Extra interest =$25.48mx6%=$1.53m Forecast PAT=$16.56m+($4.5m-$1.53m)x(1-0.2)=$18.94m Forecast EPS=$18.94m/70m=$0.2706 per share Forecast share price=$0.2706x11=$2.98 per share 得分标准： (b) (i)New PAT 0.5 (i)EPS 0.5 (i)Share price 1 2 (ii)Interest 0.5 (ii)New PAT 1 (ii)EPS 0.5 (ii)Share price 1 3 知识点标签：Business finance---Sources of,and raising,business finance---Methods of raising equity finance 难易程度：中 解析： (b)问要求计算出rights issue 和 loan notes 这两种不同的融资方式下公司的预期EPS 和股价。 这两者的区别是rights issue 属于股权融资，股票数量会增加，但不会增加利息费用。而loan notes 属于债权融资，会增加我们的利息费用，但股票数量不变。 因此在计算PAT 时 ，loan notes 需要将新增的利息1.53m(25.48m*6%) 从 PAT中扣除，但 计算EPS 时，股票数量依然是70m。而 rights issue 计算 PAT 时不扣除新增利息，但计算EPS 时股票数量增加到84m (包括 rights issue 新增发的14m)。 最后我们计算forecast share price时运用P/E ratio method,将两种方法下各自的EPS 乘以P/E ratio:11。 (c)Discuss the ways in which a company can issue new equity shares. (5 marks) 题目类型：Word 官方答案： Rights issue A rights issue involves issuing shares to the existing shareholders in proportion to their existing holding.Rights issues are often successful,easier to price and are cheaper to arrange than a public issue but the amount of finance raised is limited as there is a finite amount that shareholders will be willing to invest.A rights issue would be mandatory if shareholders have not elected to waive their pre-emptive rights. Private placing A private placing is when a company,usually with the assistance of an intermediary,seeks out new investors on a one-to-one basis.Shares are normally issued to financial institutions when performing a placing rather than to individuals.This can be a useful source of new equity for an unlisted company but control of the company will be diluted as a result.A placing is also cheaper to arrange than a public issue but only useful for relatively small issues. Public offer If the company is listed,it may undertake a public offer whereby shares are offered for sale to the public at large.This is an expensive way of issuing shares as there are significant regulatory costs involved and like the placing,control of the existing shareholders will be diluted.A public issue will,however,allow very large amounts of equity finance to be raised,and will also give a wide spread of ownership. Initial public offering (IPO) If the company is not listed,it can list through the process of an IPO which willraise equity at the same time.An IPO will be more expensive than a public offer as there are further regulations having to be complied with,increasing costs.Consequently,only a large company wishing to raise a significant amount of finance would consider this option. 得分标准： (c) Up to 2 marks per method 5 知识点标签：Business finance---Sources of,and raising,business finance---Methods of raising equity finance 难易程度：中 解析： (c)问要求讨论获得股权融资的几种方式，包括rights issue 、private placing 、public offering 以 及IPO 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 首先需要简单介绍一下这四种融资方式。 其次需要描述一下这四种方式的特征，也就是相比起其他方法其优缺点是什么。 比如 rights issue 相比起其他三种方式，更简单，成功的概率更高，成本也更低。但缺点是能 够获得的资金是有限额的。 Private placing 比起 public offering跟便宜，但融到的资金会更少。 Public offering能够获得的资金是最多的，股东分布的范围也最广。但是是最昂贵的一种方 式 。 而 IPO 是一种特别的public offering, 指的是第一次公开发行股票，其特征是相比起普通的 public offering 更加的昂贵，因为会涉及到合规成本等其他成本，但是只有IPO 之后公司才 能继续通过public offering的方式来获得资金。 (d)Discuss and recommend whether LaForge Co should raise the finance it requires by reducing its annual dividend (6 marks) 题目类型：Word 官方答案： The director's suggestion of reducing the forthcoming dividend would raise at most,$5.6m(70m x $0.08)so in itself,would not be sufficient but would provide 22%($5.6m/$25.48m)of the total required.This would reduce the amount of new external finance needing to be raised,potentially reducing financing cost,but there are further problems with this suggestion. Signalling effect The signalling argument suggests that in the absence of perfect information,for example in a semi-strong form efficient capital market,the dividend announcement will send a message or "signal"to the market.Generally,a reduction in dividend (such as proposed here)could be interpreted as bad news by investors and result in a fall in LaForge Co's share price. Clientele effect Different investors have different needs relating to income or capital growth.LaForge Co has consistently paid dividends in the past so switching to a lower/zero pay-out could alienate some shareholders,resulting in large volumes of share sales.Given the different shareholders that LaForge Co has,this could be a real issue for them. Liquidity preference Generally,it is thought that shareholders,even those who prefer low pay-outs/high reinvestment, still wish to receive some dividend now as this is a certain return compared with the more risky and uncertain future dividends or capital growth. Recommendation Given that LaForge Co is a listed company with different shareholders and has consistently paid dividends in the past,a reduction in dividend could damage shareholder relations and possibly result in reduced shareholder wealth.The reduction in dividend is not recommended. 得分标准： (d) Signalling effect 2 Clientele effect 2 Other 1 Recommendation 1 6 知识点标签：Business finance--Sources of,and raising,business finance---the relationship between dividend policy and the financing decision 难易程度：中 解析： (d)问要求讨论通过减少股息来融资的方式对LaForge 公司是否适用。 首先从可行性的角度，我们可以计算发现就算LaForge 完全不发股息，其最多可以获得的资 金为$5.6m(70mx $0.08), 而它总共需要$25.48m 的资金，也就是股息最多可以提供22%的 资金需求。所以该方式是不可行的。 除了可行性的角度外，我们也可以根据一些股息理论来分析一下减少股息的影响，包括 signalling effect\Clientele effect 以及liquidity preference。 首先从 signalling effect的角度来看，减少股息会被股东认为是一个公司未来发展不好的信息， 会影响公司的股价。 从 Clientele effect的角度看，公司原本吸引的是喜欢高股息支付率的股东们，而现在突然减 少股息会引起股东不满，抛售公司股票，从而影响股价。 从 liquidity preference的角度看，股东会更喜欢现在就能够获得的现金股息，因为现金股息 能够提供最高的流动性。 最后我们的结论，也就是题目要求的建议就是，LaForge 公司过去一直持续支付股息，贸然 减少股息会损害和股东的关系，甚至可能损害股东的财富。因此该公司不一样减少股息来进 行融资。 1 、2020 S/D Q1 本题核心知识点： Business finance (a)Evaluate whether,on financial grounds,Spine Co should finance the expansion with debt or equity. (10 marks) 题目类型：Excel 得分标准： Increased PBIT 0.5 Debt-increased interest 1 Debt-increased PBT 0.5 Debt -increased PAT 0.5 Debt-revised EPS 1 Debt-capital gain 1 Equity -increased PBT 0.5 Equity-revised EPS 0.5 Equity-TERP 1 Equity-capital gain 1 Recommendation 1 Other discussion 1 10 知识点标签：Business finance---Sources of,and raising,business finance 难易程度：高 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题问的是从财务角度评估 Spine Co 是否应该通过债务融资或股权融资的方式为扩张提 供资金。因此，我们要分别算出选择债务类融资以及权益类融资别对企业当前的财务状况所 带来的影响： 首先，我们先计算出当企业选择债务类融资时，对当前企业利息费用的支付，税前利润，税 后利润，每股收益，以及资本利得分别所产生的影响： 发生扩张之后，预测会给PBIT 带来20%的增长，因此可以算出新的息税前利润。 Increased PBIT=13.040m x 1.2=$15,648,000 Financing by debt 如果选择了债务类融资，当发行了新的债务之后，企业新的利息支付为： Current interest payment=$240,000 Increase in interest=15mx0.08=$1,200,000 Revised interest payment=1,200,000+240,000=$1,440,000 对税前和税后利润分别的影响： Revised PBT=15,648,000-1,440,000=$14,208,000 Revised PAT=14,208,000x0.7=$9,945,600 根据调整了之后的税后利润可以算出发行了新的债务融资之后的每股收益： Current EPS=8,960,000/12,000,000=$0.747 per share Revised EPS=9,945,600/12,000,000=$0.829 per share 利用当前的PER, 算出发行债务之后的股票价格，从而算出资本利得： Current PER=6.25/0.747=8.37 times Revised share price=8.37x0.829=$6.94 per share Capital gain=6.94-6.25=$0.69 per share Financing by equity 如果选择了权益类融资，当发行了新的供股之后，对每股收益的影响： Revised PBT=15,648,000-240,000=$15,408,000 Revised PAT=15,408,000x0.7=$10,785,600 Revised number of shares=12mx 1.25=15m shares Revised EPS=10,785,600/15,000,000=$0.719 利用当前的PER, 算出发行供股之后的股票价格，从而算出资本利得： Current PER=6.25/0.747=8.37 times Revised share price=8.37x0.719=$6.02 per share TERP=((4x6.25)+5.00)/5=$6.00 per share Capital gain=6.02-6.00=$0.02 per share 根据上述的计算结果，给出企业的建议是：选择通过债务类融资的方式去获取资金。因为在 债务类融资的情况下，股东的资本利得能够获得更大化。 (b)Explain and discuss the relationship between systematic risk and unsystematic risk. (5 marks) 题目类型：word 得分标准： Portfolio diversification 2 Systematic risk 1 Unsystematic risk 1 Other discussion 1 5 知识点标签：Business finance---Estimating the cost of capital ---Estimate the cost of equity including:explanation and discussion of systematic and unsystematic risk 难易程度：中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考查的是对系统分析和非系统风险定义与关系的理解。可以根据本题的分值进行答题分 析，关于风险的划分中，最重要的假设就是关于投资组合分析的假设，因此可以重点描写， 再结合题目的要求分别去阐述两类风险，以及两者之间的关系。 第一，解释一下什么是风险以及如何去降低风险。 任何投资从从预期和预期所带来的结果两个角度去分析，都存在着与实际结果可能不同的风 险。这种风险可以通过持有几个不同的投资项目，建立一个投资组合，通过分散的方式去降 低风险。 第二，解释风险中又包括系统分析和非系统风险。 建立投资组合，通过分散的方式去实现总风险的降低是有限度的。投资组合无法消除的风险 被称为系统性风险。它代表与整个金融系统有关的风险，任何投资的公司都无法避免。可以 通过投资组合分散化消除的风险称为非系统性风险或特定风险，因为它与投资的特定公司有 关。经验表明，投资20到30 家公司的股票是足以消除投资组合中几乎所有的非系统性 风险。 第三，对系统分析进行进一步的划分与阐述。 系统性风险包括经营风险和财务风险。没有债务的公司财务单独面临经营风险，而同时拥有 股权和债务融资的公司面临经营风险和财务风险。 (c)Discuss the assumptions made by the capital asset pricing model.(5 marks) (20 marks) 题目类型：word 得分标准： Diverse portfolios 1 Single-period 1 Perfect capital market 1 Risk-free rate 1 Other discussion 1 5 知 识 点 标 签： Business finance---Estimating the cost of capital ---Estimate the cost of equity including:application of the CAPM,its assumptions,advantages and disadvantages. 难易程度：中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考查的是资本资产定价模型相关的假设 可以根据本题的分值进行答题分析，建议写出四个假设，并对每个假设进行展开阐述： 投资组合理论相关的假设有：假设投资者都是持有最优分散风险投资组合，资本资产定价模 型是基于单期的模型，投资组合理论是基于完美资本市场，资本资产定价模型假设所有的投 资者都是可以以无风险收益率进行借贷的。 2 、2020 S/D Q2 本题核心知识点： Investment appraisal (a)For investment decision 1: (i)Calculate the net present value of project B;and. (4 marks) (ii)Given the capital constraint,calculate the optimum investment combination and the resulting net present value. (6 marks) 题目类型：excel 得分标准： (i)Inflated sales 1 (i)Inflated costs 1 (i)NPV 2 4 (ii)PI calculation 2 (ii)PI ranking 1 ii)Choose project E over C 1 (ii)Optimal NPV 2 6 知识点标签：Investment appraisal---Specific investment decisions (Lease or buy,asset replacement,capital rationing) 难易程度：中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： (a)(i) 本题考查的计算项目B 的净现值： 在进行NPV 计算的时候，首先要考虑的是NPV 的处理原则，也就是现金流和折现率之间的 匹配。如果题目中给出来的现金流，是需要经历不同的通货膨胀，那么这个时候就必须要将 现金流调整成考虑类通货膨胀之后的名义上的现金流。那么对应的，在折现率的选择上也应 该是选择考虑了通货膨胀之后的名义上的折现率。 通过题目信息可以知道，项目B 中，每一个变量所经历的通货膨胀是不同的，因此我们需 要在实际现金流的基础上调整通货膨胀，算出名义上的现金流。然后，在对应的选用名义上 的折现率去进行折现，算出项目B的净现值。 Year 1 2 3 4 Sales(inflated at 4%) 754 827 996 716 Costs (inflated at 5%) 152 185 234 114 Net cash flow 602 642 762 602 DCF @10% 0.909 0.826 0.751 0.683 Present Values 547 530 572 411 Total PV 2,061 Less:Initial outlay 1,500 NPV 561 (a)(ii) 本题考查的是在有限的资本限额下，为企业选出能够带来最优NPV 的项目组合： 通过题目信息可以知道，本题中的6个项目都是可分割的项目，因此可以用营盈利指数的方 法对项目进行排序，选出最优的项目组合。再在有限的资本限额中，算出能够做的项目可以 带来的NPV 的值。 首先，计算出每个项目的盈利指数和并根据盈利指数的高低进行排名。 Project Outlay $'000 NPV$'000 P.Index (NPV/Outlay) Rank A 1,000 390 390/1,000= 0.390 5 B ,500 561 561/1,500= 0.374 6 D 1,125 590 590/1,125= 0.524 1{highest} E 1,850 840 840/1,850= 0.454 3 F 1,300 635 635/1,300= 0.488 2 根据题目信息可以的得知项目C 和 E 是互斥的，因此不能一起完成，两个项目中如果选择 一个就不能够去做另外一个。在结合所有项目都是可分割的项目，可以直接用PI 指数的高 低对项目进行排序，根据计算的结果，可以选出能够产生最优NPV 的项目组合是D,F,E,A,B。 根据选出的最优组合计算出，在有限的资本限额中，这些项目能够给企业带来的最优NPV 的 值 。 Rank Project % Capital $'000 NPV$'000 1 D 100 1,125 590 2 F 100 1,300 635 3 E 100 1,850 840 4 A 72.5 725 283 5,000 p,348 最做完项E 后，只剩下$725,000的资金。因此，在有限的资金中，A 项目只能完成其中的72.5% (725/1,000=0.725),因此，对应的A 项目只能贡献粗72.5%NPV的值(0.725x$390,000= $283,000)。将所有能够完成项目所贡献出的NPV 加总之后就可以得出最大的净现值 $2,3482,000。 (b)For investment decision 2,explain the approach Crocket Co should use to determine the optimum replacement cycle for the company car fleet. (4 marks) 题目类型：word 得分标准： Determine cash flows 1 PV for each cycle 1 Calculate EAC 1 Decision rule 1 4 知识点标签：Investment appraisal ---Specific investment decisions (Lease or buy,asset replacement,capital rationing) 难易程度：高 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考察的是根据题目中信息的描述，对Crocket Co跟换车队的最佳时间给出建议并作出 相应的解释： 首先，先确定不同置换周期下3、4和5年)的现金流状况。其中包括车队的初始投资，每 年发生的维护费用，以及循环结束时车队的运行成本和剩余价值。 然后，这些现金流量需要以合适的折现率进行折现，以确定每个循环周期现金流的现值 (PV)。 所有方案下的PV 都将是负数，因为这里对比的只是车队的运营成本，与收入无关。 鉴于决策的持续替换性质，那么不同时间的替换方案需要有一个共同的时间维度。简单来说， 虽然三年进行一次置换所产生的PV 最便宜的，但它将会比未来的四年或五年的置换周期发 生更多次无限期地循环。为了考虑时间尺度的差异，每个周期的 PV 需要转换成等年发生 的成本 (EAC) 进行对比。一旦计算了每个周期的 EAC, 哪一种方案下EAC 越低，说明哪 一种方案最便宜，将是车队更换的最佳周期。 (c)In relation to investment decision 3,describe the two approaches for dealing with inflation AND provide a reasoned recommendation as to which approach Crocket Co's management should follow. (6 marks) (20 marks) 题目类型：word 得分标准： Specific inflation 1 Cost of capital 1 Nominal method 1 Real method 1 Other points 1 Recommendation 1 6 知识点标签：Investment appraisal ---Allowing for inflation and taxation in DCF---Apply and discuss the real-terms and nominal-terms approaches to investment appraisal 难易程度：中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考查的是分别描述一下处理通货膨胀的两种方法，并结合题目信息给出合理的建议，对 于 Crocket Co的管理层应该遵循其中的哪一种方法。 在做项目投资评估分析时，通货膨胀的会造成两部分的影响， 一部分是会影响项目的现金流， 另一部分就是折现率。因此，处理通货膨胀的方法分成2种，分别是实际投资评估方法和名 义投资评估方法，而两者的不同之处就在于是否将通货膨胀的影响纳入评估计算。 然后分别对实际投资评估方法和名义投资评估方法展开阐述。实际投资评估方法不考虑通货 膨胀率，其现金流和折现率都需要用real term,不考虑通货膨胀的影响。 而名义投资评估方法考虑inflation 。 其现金流需要按照specific inflation rate 进行通货膨胀， 折现率则是用general inflation rate 进行通货膨胀。 1、2021MJQ1 本题核心知识点：sources of finance (a) (i)Using the Capital Asset Pricing Model,calculate Zeddemore Co's current cost of equity and a project-specific cost of equity suitable for the New venture. (6 marks) 题目类型：excel 得分标准： Current cost of equity 0.5 WCP Co equity MV 0.5 WCP Co debt MV 0.5 Asset beta 1 Number of shares 0.5 Zeddemore Co equity MV 0.5 Zeddemore Co debt MV 0.5 Total debt 0.5 Equity beta 1 Project cost of equity 0.5 6 知识点标签： Business finance -Estimating the cost of capital-The calculationlassumptionsladvantages and disadvantages of CAPM 难易程度： 中 解析：以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 题目中， Zeddemore Co 正在考虑进军一个不属于自己原来行业的业务，根据要求，我们需 要计算出Zeddemore Co目前的 cost of equity 以及做这个新项目而产生的新 cost of equity 。 首先我们应当先正确选择cost of equity 的计算方法。根据题目所给出的条件：beta 、risk free rate of return等，我们可以判断使用CAPM 模型进行计算。 在现有条件下，我们可以先行计算Zeddemore Co自身目前的current cost of equity。 ke=4%+2·3(10%-4%)=17·8% 其中： 4 % 为 risk free rate of return 2.3为Zeddemore Co的 equity beta 10%为 return on market portfolio; 其次，就是计算第二个问题， new cost of equity; 由于进入了新的行业 Zeddemore Co原先自身的equity beta不能使用，因此我们可以先行找 到处于目标行业中的公司： WCP Co。将其equity beta进行处理： 第一步：去除 WCP Co equity beta中的杠杆： Asset beta=equity betax{Ve/(Ve+Vdx(1-t))} Ve=100mx$2·60=$260m Vd=$110m x0·96=$105·6m Asset beta=1-25x{$260m/($260m+$105·6mx(1-0·2))}=0·94 其中： Ve 为 WCPCo 公司权益资本部分的价值； Vd 为 WCP Co 公司债务资本部分的价值； 第二步：将 Zeddemore Co自身的杠杆加载进asset beta Ve=$40m/$0·50x$1·05=$84m Vd(Loan notes)=$250m x0·65=$162·5m Vd(Loan)=$20m Total Vd=$162·5m+$20m=$182·5m Equity beta=asset betax {(Ve+Vd(1-t)/Ve} Equity beta=0·94x{($84m+$182·5mx(1-0·2))/$84m}=2·57 其中： Ve 为Zeddemore Co 公司权益资本部分的价值； Vd 为 Zeddemore Co 公司债务资本部分的价值； 第三步：代入CAPM 的公式，求解new cost of equity ke =4%+2·57x(10%-4%)=19·42% (ii)Referring to your calculation above,comment briefly on the view of the commercial director. (2 marks) 题目类型：word 得分标准： Higher risk/return 1 Wrong decision 1 2 知识点标签： Business finance -Estimating the cost of capital -The calculationlassumptionsladvantages and disadvantages of CAPM 难易程度： 中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 该题分值一共两分， 一分可以用来描述风险与回报之间的关系，另一分通过结合题意来给出 相关答案。 (b)Discuss THREE problems Zeddemore Co may be facing as a result of its current high level of gearing. (6 marks) 题目类型：wo rd 得分标准： Problem 1 2 Problem 2 2 Problem 3 2 6 知识点标签： Business finance -Sources of finance and their relative costs -Problems of high levels of gearing 难易程 度： 低 解析：以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 该题分值一共6分，我们共计需要写出3点与gearing problem 有关的问题，包括但不限于： (1)increased earnings volatility: 杠杆过高的情况下，高额的利息支付对收益产生的不利影 响； (2)Cost of equity finance: 权益融资的成本，会随着杠杆的不断增加而出现增加； (3)借债能力：杠杆过高导致公司出现失衡的资本结构，这可能会侵蚀企业后续的借债能 力； (4)破产风险：过高的杠杆可能会出现过高的利息，使得企业入不敷出，进而出现破产的 相关风险； (c)In respect of both equity and debt,discuss the risk-return relationship and how it affects Zeddemore Co's financing costs. objective of working capital management;Bulk purchase discount;early settlement discount (b) (i)Calculate the financial effects on Kandy Co of accepting the bulk purchase discount.(6 marks) 题目类型：word 官方答案： (a)(i)Bulk purchase discount Current policy: Annual ordering cost=18,000/1,000x$75=$1,350 per year Buffer inventory =18,000x2/360=100 units Average inventory holding=(1,000/2)+100=600 units Annual holding cost=600×$30×0·09=$1,620 per year Purchase cost=18,000x$30=$540,000 per year Total cost=$1,350+$1,620+$540,000=$542,970 per year Bulk purchase discount: Purchase price=$30x(1-0·015)=$29·55 per unit Order cost=$75+$25=$100 per order Annual ordering cost=18,000/1,500x$100=$1,200 per year Average inventory holding=(1,500/2)+100=850 units Annual holding cost=850x$29·55x0·10=$2,512 per year Purchase cost=18,000x$29·55=$531,900 per year Total cost=$1,200+$2,512+$531,900=$535,612 per year 得分标准： Order cost(current) 0 ·5 Average inventory(current) 1 Holding cost(current) 0 ·5 Purchase cost(current) 0 ·5 New purchase price/order 0 ·5 New ordering cost/order 0 ·5 Order cost(new) 0 ·5 Average inventory(new) 1 Holding cost(new) 0 ·5 Purchase cost(new) 0 ·5 Total 6 知识点标签：Working capital management-Management of inventories,accounts receivable,accounts payable and cash-Early settlement and bulk purchase discounts 高顿教育 难易程度：中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题询问如果采取了大批量采购的方式，对于企业会产生怎样的财务影响： 首先，同学们需要了解，财务影响具体的体现就是成本的增加或者减少，因此该题目需要将 现有未采取大批量采购的情况下的总成本和选择了大批量采购下的总成本进行对比，最终判 断出采取大批量采购能够获得的好处： Total cost=ordering cost+holding cost+purchase cost 未采取大批量采购下的总成本： Annual ordering cost=18,000/1,000x$75=$1,350 per year 此处，根据题意，年总需求为18,000件，每次订购1000件，共订购18次，每次订单成本$75, 因此年总订单成本为$1350。 Buffer inventory =18,000x2/360=100 units 基于防止缺货的考量，公司需要持有部分安全存货，且题目明确说明持有的安全存货可供企 业使用2天， 一年为360天，因此2天的量即为年总需求乘以2/360。 Average inventory holding=(1,000/2)+100=600 units 基于以上信息，企业持有的平均存货数量即为600件，此处需注意，安全存货是一直存在于 企业的，所以不需要除以2。 Annual holding cost=600x$30x0·09=$1,620 per year 持有存货的单位成本为采购价格的9%,因此总的持有成本为$1620。 Purchase cost=18,000x$30=$540,000 per year 因此，总成本为： Total cost=$1,350+$1,620+$540,000=$542,970 per year 当企业采取大批量采购，总成本为： Purchase price=$30x(1-0·015)=$29·55 per unit 大批量采购下，企业的采购价格折扣为1.5%,因此单位采购价格降低为$29.55 Order cost=$75+$25=$100 per order 每次采购的订单成本则由于每次采购量的增加，而上升了$25,因此为$100每次。 Annual ordering cost=18,000/1,500x$100=$1,200 per year Average inventory holding=(1,500/2)+100=850 units Annual holding cost=850x$29·55x0·10=$2,512 per year 企业的单位持有成本，改变为此时采购价格的10%,因此年持有成本为$2512。 Purchase cost=18,000x$29·55=$531,900 per year 因此，在大批量采购下，总成本为：Total cost=$1,200+$2,512+$531,900=$535,612 per year (a) (ii)Calculate the financial effects on Kandy Co of offering the early settlement discount. (4 marks) 题目类型：word 官方答案： (a)(ii)Early settlement discount Current situation: 30-day receivables=$45mx0·6x 30/360=$2,250,000 40-day receivables=$45mx0·4x40/360=$2,000,000 Total receivables =$4,250,000 Current finance cost=$4,250,000x0·07=$297,500 After introduction of discount: 10-day trade receivables =$45mx0·5x10/360=$625,000 30-day trade receivables=$45mx0·2x30/360=$750,000 40-day trade receivables=$45mx0·3x40/360=$1,500,000 Total receivables=$2,875,000 Finance cost=$2,875,000x0·07=$201,250 per year Discount cost=$45m x0·005x0·5=$112,500 per year Total cost=$201,250+$112,500=$313,750 per year 得分标准： Purchase cost(new) l Finance cost (current) 0.5 Trade receivables (new) 1 Finance cost (new) 0.5 Discount cost JCATIO 1 Total 4 知识点标签：Working capital management-Management of inventories,accounts receivable,accounts payable and cash-Early settlement and bulk purchase discounts 难易程度： 中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题思路和上一小问一样，通过对比在现有情形下，企业由于应收账款这项被顾客占用的资 金，产生的财务费用，和采取折扣后，企业仍然需要发生的总成本进行对比。具体计算如下： Current situation: 30-day receivables=$45mx0·6x 30/360=$2,250,000 40-day receivables=$45mx0·4x40/360=$2,000,000 此处由于应收账款分为两部分收回，对应不同的天数，因此需要分开计算。 Total receivables=$4,250,000 Current finance cost=$4,250,000x0·07=$297,500 After introduction of discount: 10-day trade receivables =$45mx0·5x10/360=$625,000 30-day trade receivables=$45mx0·2x 30/360=$750,000 40-day trade receivables=$45mx0·3x40/360=$1,500,000 此处由于采取折扣之后，应收账款分为三部分收回，对应不同的天数，因此需要分开计算。 Total receivables=$2,875,000 Finance cost=$2,875,000x0·07=$201,250 per year Discount cost=$45mx0·005×0·5=$112,500 per year 采取折扣之后，企业不仅有财务费用，还有给出折扣的成本，因此此时的总成本=财务费用 +折扣成本。 Total cost=$201,250+$112,500=$313,750 per year (a)(iii)Using your result form (i)and (ii)above,comment on the financial acceptability to Kandy Co of each option.(2 marks) 题目类型：word 官方答案： The bulk purchase discount is financially acceptable as it willreduce gimble stocking costs by $7,358 per year($542,970-$535,612). The early settlement discount is not financially acceptable as it will increase costs by $16,250 ($313,750-$297,500)per year. 总体财务影响，对比原本的总成本$542,970和采取大批量采购下的成本$535,612,公司采 取大批量采购能够每年节省$7,358的存货成本。 得分标准： Bulk purchase 1 Early settlement discount 工 Total 2 知识点标签：Working capital management-Management of inventories,accounts receivable,accounts payable and cash-Early settlement and bulk purchase discounts receivable&inventory 难易程度：低 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 分别将大批量采购后的成本和采取大批量采购前的成本进行对比，算出财务影响。以及分别 将实行早付款折扣后会发生的成本和采取早付款折扣前发生的成本进行对比，算出财务影响。 (b)Discuss the working capital objectives of liquidity and profitability and the conflict between the objectives.(4 marks) 题目类型：word 官方答案： (b)The two main objectives of working capital management are to ensure profitability(generating return for investors)while at the same time ensuring liquidity(meeting its obligations when they fall due).In other words,it is about getting the right balance between current assets and current liabilities. Having high levels of current assets can help in meeting short-term liabilities,particularly if there is a high cash balance.Good liquidity is important as it is cash which settles the liabilities,not profit.Profitable companies can still fail due to poor liquidity in the short term. However,the problem with high levels of current assets is that they are not profitable.Cash,for example,does not generate a high return,if indeed any return at all.Holding inventory is similarly not generating any return for the company,only the sale of inventory generates the profitability. Carrying lower levels of current assets with more cash invested in long-term,non-current assets could generate higher returns for the company but would impact its ability to settle its liabilities. Consequently,there is a conflict,or trade off,between profitability and liquidity. The bulk purchase discount Kandy Co is being offered is a good example of this conflict.By accepting the discount and buying in larger quantities,the cost is reduced(as demonstrated in a(i), increasing profitability.But this will mean less cash in the bank due to higher levels of inventory holding.This lower level of cash will affect Kandy Co's ability to pay its current liabilities,thus liquidity will be affected. This conflict between the two objectives is inevitable and there is no correct answer as to the level of current assets and liabilities to hold.It is for every business to determine itself. 得分标准： Liquidity l Profitability 1 Conflict between them p Total 4 知识点标签：Working capital management-The nature,elements and importance of working capital-Objectives of working capital management 难易程度： 中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 首先营运资本管理的两个目标：流动性和盈利性的含义，注意具体解释流动性和盈利性。其 次具体分析这两个目标之间存在的冲突，注意此处需要具体举例说明。最后结合题目案例具 体说明案例中营运资本管理存在的冲突。 具体翻译如下： 营运资本管理的两个主要目标是确保盈利能力(为投资者创造回报),同时确保流动性(在 到期时履行其义务)。换句话说，它是关于在流动资产和流动负债之间取得适当的平衡。 拥有高水平的流动资产有助于满足短期负债，特别是在现金余额高的情况下。良好的流动性 很重要，因为负债是用现金结算，而不是用利润。在短期内，如果流动性差，即便是盈利的 公司仍然有可能倒闭。 然而，如果流动资产持有过高的问题在于它们无利可图。例如，现金即便产生汇报，也不会 产生高回报。单纯持有存货同样不会为公司产生任何回报，只有销售存货才能产生盈利。携 带较低水平的流动资产，将更多现金投资于长期非流动资产，可以为公司带来更高的回报， 但会影响其清偿债务的能力。因此，盈利能力和流动性之间存在冲突或权衡。 Kandy 公司提供的批量购买折扣就是这种冲突的一个很好的例子。通过接受折扣并大量购 买，成本降低(如a(i) 所示),从而提高盈利能力。但这将意味着由于存货持有水平较高， 企业持有的现金减少。这种较低的现金水平将影响 Kandy 公司支付其流动负债的能力，从 而影响流动性。 这两个目标之间的这种冲突是不可避免的，对于持有的流动资产和负债水平没有正确的答案。 每个企业都要根据自己的情况决定。 (c)Explain the cash operating cycle and discuss its relationship with the level of investment in working capital. (4 marks) (20 marks) 题目类型：word 官方答案： The cash operating cycle refers to the average length oftime it takes for a business to generate cash having paid for an item of inventory.In other words,having paid for the inventory,the cash operating cycle is the length of time it remains in inventory before being sold and then how long until the customer pays for it and cash is returned to the bank account. It is calculated by adding the average inventory holding period and the average trade receivables collection period and then subtracting the average trade payables payment period. The cash operating cycle is effectively measuring the business'liquidity in terms of cash generation-a long cash operating cycle would indicate potential liquidity problems as the business is without cash for a long time. The relationship of the cash operating cycle with the investment in working capital is that higher levels of investment will generally increase the cash operating cycle.High inventory levels will usually mean longer inventory holding periods,increasing the cash operating cycle.High trade receivables are usually due to longer collection periods for collecting in debt,again increasing the cycle. Finally,paying suppliers very quickly will increase the investment in working capital due to low levels of trade payables,but this will again increase the cash cycle. 得分标准： Explanation of cycle 2 Link to investment 2 Total 4 知识点标签：Working capital management-Management of inventories,accounts receivable,accounts payable and cash-Cash operating cycle 难易程度： 中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 首先解释什么是现金经营周期；其次表述现金经营周期的具体计算方法；然后说明现金经营 周期能够在一定程度上反映企业潜在的流动性问题；最后具体分析现金经营周期和企业营运 资本投资水平之间的具体关系。 具体解释如下： 现金运营周期是指企业在支付了存货投入经营之后，到收回现金所需的平均时间长度。换句 话说，在支付了存货后(即payable days为0),现金经营周期是它在存货卖出去之前停留的 时间长度 (inventory days),然后是客户付钱后将钱存到银行的时间长度 (receivable days)。 补充，如果payable days不为0,应该要减去payable days。 它的计算方法是将应收账款周转天数+存货周转天数-应付账款周转天数。 现金运营周期有效地衡量了企业在现金生成方面的流动性——较长的现金经营周期表明 存在潜在的流动性问题，因为企业长期没有现金。 现金经营周期与营运资金投入的关系是，投资水平越高，现金经营周期越大。高存货水平通 常意味着更长的存货持有期，从而增加现金运营周期。高贸易应收账款通常是由于收取债务 的回收期较长，再次增加了周期。 最后，过快地支付资金给供应商，会增加营运资本的投资水平，应付账款减少，将会减少应 付账款周转天数，进而增加现金经营周期。 2021 DSQ2 本题核心知识点： Leasing and borrowing 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 题目要求选择出到底是直接租赁还是借钱之后购买该设备。因此要分别计算出两种选择会发 生的成本的现值，然后选择成本较小的方式作为企业的采购方式，每种方式具体需要注意的 点如下： Option 1:Borrow to buy 固定资产折旧抵税好处 (TAD)= 固定资产折旧费用*税率。固定资产折旧=(成本-残值)/ 使用年份，即depreciation=(34000-14000)/4=$5000, 则折旧抵税为$5000*20%=$1000 Emission tax 作为费用有节税作用，节省的税费为：600*20%=$120 Lease or buy中使用的折现率均为利率，即题目给出的4%。 Option 2:Lease 税费原本是在第一年年底产生，但是由于题目明确说明，交税时间递延一年，因此对应的节 税作用也递延一年，因此$1200的租金节税作用从第二年开始。 最后根据比较，租赁的成本会更低，因此选择租赁。 (c)Discuss TWO reasons (other than possible after-tax cost advantages )why Hawker Co may choose to lease rather than buy the new delivery vehicle. 题目类型：Word 官方答案： Reasons,other than after-tax cost,for leasing -Leasing can offer more flexibility than borrowing and buying.Lease periods can be for less than an asset's useful life,or break clauses may be offered by the lessor.If technology changes and the asset becomes out of date before the end of its expected life,the lessee does not have to keep on using it. -Under the borrow and buy option,Hawker Co would carry the risk of the vehicle failing to achieve a disposal value of $14,000.If leased,this risk would be transferred to the lessor. -The lessee may not have enough cash to pay for the vehicle and might have difficulty obtaining a bank loan.If this is the case,leasing may be the only way of getting use of the asset. (Note:Only two reasons were required) 得分标准： I reason 2 2nd reason 2 Total 4 知识点标签：Investment appraisal-Specific investment decisions(Lease or buy,asset replacement,capital rationing)-Leasing and borrowing to buy 难易程度：中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 原因1:租赁相比购买会更加灵活，因为租赁期一般小于设备的寿命，如果有新的技术出现， 企业可以尽快更换新的设备。 原因2:在采购下，会有残值没有办法完全实现的风险，而租赁下，固定资产并不属于企业， 因此不存在残值相关的风险。 原因3: 在企业资金困难的时候，租赁可以用更少的钱就能够使用固定资产。 以上三个原因，选择两个描述即可。 (d)Discuss THREE advantages of using NPV rather 题目类型：Word 官方答案： Advantages of NPV over IRR in investment appraisal than IRR in investment appraisal. In most simple accept or reject decisions,IRR and NPV will select the same project.However, NPV has certain advantages over IRR. The NPV of a proposed project,if calculated at an appropriate cost of capital,is equal to the increase in shareholder wealth which the project offers.In this way,NPV is directly linked to the assumed objective of the company,the maximisation of shareholder wealth.IRR calculates the rate of return on projects,and although this can show the attractiveness of the project to shareholders,it does not measure the absolute increase in wealth which the project offers. NPV looks at absolute increases in wealth and thus can be used to compare projects of different sizes.IRR looks at relative rates of return and in doing so ignores the size of the investment projects. NPV is not subject to the technical difficulties which limit the usefulness of the IRR method. First,in situations involving multiple reversals in project cash flows,it is possible that the IRR method may produce multiple IRRs (that is,there can be more than one interest rate which would produce an NPV of zero).If decision makers are aware of the existence of multiple IRRs,it is still possible for them to make the correct accept or reject decision using IRR,but if unaware,they could make the wrong decision. Second,in situations of mutually exclusive projects,it is possible that the IRR method will (incorrectly)rank projects in a different order to the NPV method.This is due to the inbuilt reinvestment assumption of the IRR method.The IRR method assumes that any net cash inflows generated during the life of the project willbe reinvested at the project's IRR.NPV,on the other hand,assumes a reinvestment rate equal to the cost of capital.Generally NPV's assumed reinvestment rate is more realistic and hence it ranks projects correctly. Finally,NPV can be used in situations where the cost of capital changes from year to year. Although IRR can be calculated in these circumstances,it can be difficult to make accept or reject decisions as it is difficult to know which cost of capital to compare it with. 得分标准： 1st reason 2 2nd reason 2 3rd reason 2 Total 6 知识点标签：Investment appraisal-Investment appraisal techniques-Relative merits of NPV and IRR 难易程度：中 解析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 首先说明，在决定是否接受项目的时候，NPV 和 IRR 做出的决策是相同的。但是总体而言， NPV 相比IRR, 仍然是有许多优点的，具体主要有以下： 首先，在NPV 的计算中，只要折现率 (cost of capital) 使用得当，NPV 是完全和股东财富 的增加是一致的。 其次，且NPV 着眼于财富的绝对增长，因此可用于比较不同规模的项目。IRR 着眼于相对 回报率，并在此过程中忽略了投资项目的规模。 最后，在计算上，IRR 存在的很多困难，NPV 都是没有的。具体如下：

stem_zh: 1. 在涉及项目现金流多次逆转的情况下，IRR 方法可能会产生多个IRR

2. 在互斥项目的情况下，IRR 方法可能会(错误地)以与NPV 方法不同的顺序对项目进 行排名 3.NPV 可用于资本成本每年变化的情况。虽然IRR 可以在这些情况下计算，但很难做出 接受或拒绝的决定，因为很难知道与哪种资本成本进行比较。 1、2022 MJ Q1 本 题 核 心 知 识 点：Investment appraisal (a)For the proposed investment in the new motorcycle,calculate the following: (i)Net present value; (5 marks) 题 目 类 型：Excel 官 方 答 案： Year 0 1 2 3 4 $m $m $m $m Revenue 500 ·00 954 ·00 898 ·88 476 ·40 Variable costs $m (350 ·00) (667 ·80) (629 ·20) (333 ·48) Contribution Fixed costs 150 ·00 (120 ·00) 286 ·20 (132 ·00) 269 ·68 (145 ·20) 142 ·92 (159 ·72) Investment (220 ·00) 30 ·00 Net cash flow (220 ·00) 30 ·00 154 ·20 124 ·48 13 ·20 Discount at 14% 1 ·000 0 ·877 0 ·769 0 ·675 0 ·592 Present values NPV=$16·72m Workings Calculation of income Year Inflated selling price($/unit) Demand (units/yr) Revenue($ m/yr) Calculation of variable costs (220 ·00) 26 ·31 1 20,000 25,000 500·00 118 ·58 2 21,200 45,000 954·00 84 ·02 3 22,472 40,000 898·88 7 ·81 4 23,820 20,000 476·40 Year Inflated variable cost ($/unit) Demand(units/yr) Variable cost ($m/yr) 得 分 标 准： Income Variable costs Fixed costs Initial investment Residual value PVs and NPV 1 14,000 25,000 350·00 2 14,840 45,000 667·80 3 15,730 40,000 629·20 4 16,674 20,000 333·48 1 1 工 0·5 0·5 1 知 识 点 标 签：DInvestment appraisal-1.Investment appraisal techniques -Net present value 难易程度：中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化 ， 可供同学参考 ： 本题考查的计算新的四年期项目的净现值 ： 在进行NPV 计算的时候，首先要考虑的是NPV 的处理原则，也就是现金流和折现率之间的 匹配。如果题目中给出来的现金流，是需要按照不同的特定通货膨胀率通胀调增，此时就必 须要将现金流按照特定的通胀率调整成名义现金流。与之对应的，在折现率的选择上也应该 是选择考虑了通货膨胀之后的名义折现率 。 通过题目信息可以知道，项目中，销售收入和变动成本的特定通胀率是6%,固定成本的特 定通胀率是10%,由于各个变量所经历的通货膨胀是不同的，因此我们需要在实际现金流 高顿教育 的基础上调整通货膨胀，算出名义上的现金流。需要注意的是，题目条件直接给与的是year 1的现金流，不是year 0当年的，所以后续year 2/3/4年的现金流是建立在现有year 1现金 流的基础上，按照对应的专属通胀率进行通胀调整，目前题目提供的通胀率都是年化的，所 以逐年调增。以 inflated selling price为例，year 1的是20000,year 2的为20000*(1+6%) =21200,year 3的为21200*(1+6%),即每一年的都是基于前一年的数据乘以(1+通胀率)。 所有相关现金流加总后，由于都是名义现金流，所以选用名义折现率去进行折现，算出项目 的净现值NPV。 (ii)Internal rate of return; explanation_zh: 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考查的计算新的四年期项目的内含报酬率IRR: 如果按照线性插值法计算，由于(a)(i)小问已经是按照14%的折现率得出了一个NPV=16.72, 所以只需要再任选一个折现率得出另一个NPV, 代入插值法的计算公式： 出的IRR 的计算结果会和17.82%不一样，不一样不 一定是错误的，考官会根据你取用的比率验证你计算结果正确与否进行给分。 如果你使用的是Excel 的 IRR 计算公式进行的计算，也是正确的，用Excel 的 IRR 计算公式 计算出的结果是17.70%。 (ii)Payback period;and (1 mark) 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： 高顿教育 Year 0 1 2 3 $m $m $m $m Net cash flow (220·00) 30·00 154·20 124·48 Cumulative cash flow (190·00) Payback period =2+(12x(35·80/(35·80+88·68)))=2 years 3 Or payback period =2+(35·80/(35·80+88·68))=2·3 years (35·80) months 88·68 得分标准： Payback 1 知识点标签：DInvestment appraisal-1.Investment appraisal techniques-Payback period 难易程度：低 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考查的计算新的四年期项目的投资回收期。 投资回收期就是在计算项目收回初始投资$220m 需要的年份数。根据项目的净相关现金流入， 可以逐年计算出每年还剩多少没有收回，会发现项目第二年年底还剩下$35.8m 的初始投资 没有收回，而项目第三年的现金流入是超过这个金额的，假设现金是匀速流入公司，所以是 在第三年的35.80/124.48=28.8%约等于30%的时候收回项目的的初始投资，所以项目的投资 回收期是2 . 3年。 (iv)Return on capital 题目类型：Excel 官方答案： employed (accounting rate of return)based on average investment (3 marks) Total cash inflow =$30·00m+$154·20m+$124·48m+$(13·20-30)m =$291 ·88m Total depreciation =$220m-$30m=$190m Total accounting profit =$291·88m-$190m=$101 ·88m Average accounting profit =$101 ·88m/4=$25·47m Average investment =($220m+$30m)/2)=$125m ROCE=($25·47m/$125m)x100%=20·4% 得分标准： Average accounting profit Average investment ROCE 工工工 知识点标签：DInvestment appraisal-1.Investment appraisal techniques-Return on capital employed(accounting rate of return) 难易程度：中 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题考查的按照平均投资法计算新的四年期项目的资本回报率ROCE: ROCE=Average annual accounting profit/average investment 分子部分需要用的是项目4年的年均会计利润，可以根据(a)(i) 小问中现有的项目每年的净现 高顿教育 金流入进行调整，将非现金流项目折旧(项目初始投资和项目残值之间的差额就是折旧)从 中扣除，得到项目四年总的会计利润，除以4得出年均会计利润。 分母部分，由于题目要求按照平均投资法，所以以初始投资和项目残值两者之和的平均值作 为分母部分进行计算。 (b)Discuss the suitability of the techniques used in(a)for determining whether to undertake an investment and advice whether Melplash Co's proposed investment in financially acceptable. (9 marks) 题目类型：Word 官方答案： Net present value(NPV) NPV uses cash flows rather than subjective profits.It takes into account the time value of money and the whole investment ife.It is an absolute measure,enabling better business planning.The cost of capital it uses takes into account the risk of the investment and its financing.However,the results of the NPV calculation will depend on whether the cost of capital has been accurately estimated. If the options which the board is considering are mutually exclusive,NPV can be used to rank them,as it indicates the effect on shareholder wealth.However,if shareholders have a short time horizon,they may not be prepared to wait for an investment to generate returns over its later life,even if it does have the best NPV. Internal rate of return(IRR) IRR takes into account the time value of money,cash flows and the whole life of the investment.However,whilst IRR can be calculated without knowing the company's cost of capital,a benchmark is needed to determine whether the IRR is acceptable or not. If IRR is being used for comparison,it does not indicate which investment will generate more wealth for shareholders.It may give a different result to NPV because rather than being an indicator of wealth creation,it shows how sensitive the investment is to changes in the cost of capital.In some situations,there is more than one IRR. Payback Payback has the advantage of using cash flows.Itindicates when the investment will break even and whether this projected cash flows become very uncertain.However,payback ignores the cash flows after the payback period,which for years 3- here are a significant proportion of total cash flows.It also ignores the total return on the investment.The simple payback ignores the time value of money,although a discounted payback figure can be calculated.The payback target is subjective Using payback to compare investments may result in choosing shorter-term investments giving immediate returns,rather than investments offering greater returns in the longer term.However,investments with a shorter period for recouping the amount invested may be preferred if Melplash Co has liqudity problems. Return on capital employed(ROCE) The ROCE method may reflect the wishes of shareholders that the company generates suficient profits and does consider the entire life of the investment.However,the choice of an acceptable rate is subjective,here the justification for choosing the 20%figure is unclear.ROCE does not take into account the time value of money.It is possible that an investment which increases company value will be rejected if its ROCE is below the target ROCE.The acceptability of the ROCE may also depend on the assumptions about realisable value at the end of the project. As ROCE is a percentage measure,it can be used to compare this investment with other options.However,the ROCE does not indicate the absolute change in shareholder wealth generated by different investments. Advice The investment is acceptable using three of the four methods,but fails the payback test which the directors have set.However, the directors should choose to go ahead with the investment if they can,on the basis that it has a positive NPV and therefore will increase long-term shareholder wealth.If the directors are concerned about the uncertainties of the income in later years,they should carry out sensitivity analysis. 得分标准： NPV 2 IRR 2 Payback 2 ROCE 2 Advice 1 知 识 点 标 签： DInvestment appraisal-1.Investment appraisal techniques-Net present value; Internal rate of return;Payback period;Return on capital employed (accounting rate of return) 难易程度：高 解 析： 高顿教盲 BoL pcAToN 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题一共有两个要求，一是讨论考上述四种投资评估方法的适用性，二是根据上述四种投资 评估方法得出的结果进行判断，提供是否进行投资的建议。 第一个要求讨论上述投资评估方法的适用性，其实就是需要分别对于上述四种投资评估方法 的优缺点进行讨论说明。 NPV 净现值法基于现金流而不是会计利润，而且考虑了货币的时间价值，是一种绝对值指 标。计算中，用折现率的大小反映项目面临的风险高低。能够很好的与股东财富最大化这个 目标保持一致，所以是几种方法中最好的投资评估工具。 IRR 内含报酬率是一个相对值指标，所以判断是否投资需要与公司的资金成本进行比较判断， 所以这个工具更多的是用于反映项目资金成本可以接受的变化范围，毕竟资金成本越高，项 目的净现值算出来就越小。 Payback 投资回收期是基于现金额而不是会计利润进行评估的，所以会比基于会计利润的投 资评估方法要靠谱得多。不过由于他没有考虑货币的时间价值，所以折现回收期会比投资回 收期更可靠。 ROCE 投资回报率这个投资评估工具反映了项目可以创造的会计利润的收益水平高低，计算 依据是会计利润，所以会计利润这个指标存在的问题，这个投资评估工具都具有。 第二个要求对于投资评估的计算结果对于项目投资与否提供建议： 根据NPV 净现值法的计算结果，由于是大于零的结果，所以项目值得投资。 根据IRR 的计算结果，由于是大于项目的名义折现率14%,所以项目值得投资。 根据Payback 投资回收期的计算结果，由于该项目的收回初始投资需要的时间超过了目标回 收期2年，所以根据这个投资评估方法得出的结论是这个项目不值得投资。 根据ROCE 的计算结果，由于该项目的资本回报率高于目标资本回报率，所以项目值得投 资 。 公司的管理层在进行项目投资与否的最终判断时，需要综合评估这个投资评估方法的计算结 果和方法本身的优缺点来进行最终判断，同时需要考虑项目未来的风险和不确定性对于项目 结果的影响。 2 、 2022 MJ Q2 本题核心知识点：Business finance (a)Calculate the after-tax weighted average cost of capital for Tanza Co,at 31December 20X6, on a market value basis. 解题思路首先是分析清楚公司的资本有哪几部分构成，分别计算这几个部分的资本成本和市 场价值。 根据公司的资本结构，目前公司的资本由普通股，可转换债券和银行贷款三部分构成。 ●普通股 对于普通股的资本成本，按照题目条件给出的股息信息，需要按照股息估值模型计算权益资 金成本的价值，计算时是依据20X6 和 2 0X7 两年的股息派发水平预测增长率 g。 股票的市场价值，则是用股价$5.55乘以股票数量计算得出。 ●可转换债券 对于可转换债券的资本成本，需要先推导出可转债投资者在持有可转换债券期间可以获得现 金流(需要判断可转债到期那年是转换成股票还是按照约定的名义价值赎回),然后站在融 资方的角度进行评估，计算方法是和IRR 的计算方法一致的线性插值法，任取两个折现率 对于上述现金流进行折现推导。由于year 1-3每年的现金流是interest*(1-Tax rate), 即税后 化的现金流，所以计算出来直接是税后化的资本成本。 可转债的市场价值，则是用每支名义价值$100的可转换债券按照市场价值$108.51乘以数量 进行推导。 ●银行贷款 对于银行贷款，其债务资本成本直接用利率乘以(1-Tax rate)。 银行贷款的市场价值，题目没有提供其他的信息，就默认等于银行贷款的账面价值。 各个成分的市场价值和资本成本计算完成后，就以市场价值为权重，乘以各自的资本成本， 计算出企业目前税后的加权平均资本成本。 (b) (i)Critically discuss,with reference to the relevant theory,the views of Director Aand Director B on raising new finance. (6 marks) 题目类型：Word 官方答案： 高顿教育 Director A Director A is sugesting that Modigliani &Miller's(M&M)theory of capital structure is most relevant.M&M suggested that a company's weighted average cost of capital (WACC)is primarily determined by its business activities rather than its capital structure. They also proposed that the only way a company's capital structure can affect the WACC and therefore the value of the company is by introducing more debt and thereby increasing the tax shield (the present value of the tax savings made because interest is tax-deductible whereas dividends are not). It is based on this theory that Director A is suggesting a much more aggressive regearing of Tanza Co. The problems or risks that this would pose: - Providers of debt finance(e.g.banks)are not likely to tolerate such high levels of capital gearing as it increases the risk of default.If the banks are willing to lend at all,they are likely to require a much higher interest rate to compensate them for higher credit risk. - It is possible that Tanza Co exhausts its taxable profits with deductions for interest costs. Director B Director B appears to be supporting the traditional theory by suggesting that there is an optimum.Because the cost of debt is generally lower than that of equity,by increasing gearing,Tanza Co's WACC would fall.However,as borrowing gets larger, equity investors see their financial risk increasing as more and more debt interest has to be paid before they receive a return on their equity.To compensate for this increased financial risk,the returns demanded by equity holders increase.As gearing continues to increase,the benefit of cheap debt finance is eventually outweighed by the cost of more expensive equity and WACC starts to rise.The optimal gearing level is achieved when WACC has stopped falling but has not yet started rising. The problem with this is that the optimum level of gearing can only be estimated by trial and error. 得 分 标 准： Director A 3 Director B 3 知 识 点 标 签： E Business finance-4.Capital structure theories and practical considerations - Views of Miller and Modigliani on capital structure(without tax)and its assumptions;Views of Miller and Modigliani on capital structure(with tax)and its assumptions;Traditional view of capital structure and its assumptions 难易程度：难 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题的要求是根据相关理论批判性地讨论A 经理和B 经理对于融资方式的建议。 解题思路是需要结合Tanza 公司目前的具体融资需求来分析AB 两位经理的融资观点的合理 与否。既然是批判性讨论，还要求结合相关理论，就既要说明这个观点与那个理论有关系， 还需要说明这种融资方式是否可以有效解决Tanza 公司目前的具体融资需求，可能存在的问 题 有 哪 些 。 Tanza公司目前的需要尽快筹集$200m 的长期资金支持新项目的投资需求。 A 经理的观点是借债，理由是更高的杠杆可以降低加权平均资本成本。 A 经理的这个观点和 MM with tax 理论的结论相近，所以讨论的时候需要讨论这个理论的观 点和结论，还需要说明这个理论的潜在假设与Tanza 公司目前的实际情况是否一致，存在的 潜在问题可能有哪些。 B 经理的观点是杠杆如果像行业内其他公司那样高一些可以让公司的加权平均资本成本更 低一些，不过杠杆还是不能太高，这会让公司的加权平均资本成本再次变高。 B 经理的这个观点和传统理论 traditional view是基本一致的。所以讨论的时候需要说明传统 理论的观点和结论，还要说明这个理论存在的实际操作层面的问题。 (i)Discuss TWO other factors for Tanza Co to consider in making the decision to raise debt finance or equity finance. (4 marks) 高顿教盲 oLp EpucATio 题目类型：Word 官方答案： Other factors to consider: - The finance is needed quickly,this would favour the use of debt which is less time consuming to raise.Because of this and other administrative factors,it is also less expensive to raise. - The cost of new debt may be lowered if Tanza Co has any non-current assets to offer as security.Using non-current assets as collateral will lower the risk for the lender. - It is important to consider the clientele effect too.The current shareholder-base seems to be comfortable with a gearing level which is lower than average for the industry.By changing that policy,this may change the investors'perceived risk exposure,leading to the sale of their shares. 得分标准： Factor 1 2 Factor 2 2 知识点标签：EBusiness finance-3.Sources of finance and their relative costs-Relative costs of equity and debt;The connection of creditor hierarchy and the relative costs of sources of finance; The impact of sources of finance on financial position,financial risk and shareholder wealth; 难易程度：难 解 析： 以下是对官方答案的思路简化，可供同学参考： 本题的要求是讨论 Tanza 公司在进行债务融资或者权益融资的时候需要考虑的其他意思。 解题思路是需要结合Tanza 公司目前的实际情况来分析融资时需要考虑的因素，这些因素得 是(b)(i) 中还没有涉及的内容。 4分的题目，2分 一个点，所以需要讨论至少2个点。标答提供了3个点的内容，不过正确 答案不局限于上述3点内容，言之有理都是可以得到分数的。 2022 SD Q1 Purdy Co 本题核心知识点：Working capital management This scenario relates to five requirements. Purdy Co uses 5 million litres of chemical X per year in its plastic manufacturing business. Chemical X costs Purdy Co $2.50 per litre per year to store and each order requires a quality check that costs Purdy Co $400 per order to perform. Purdy Co currently purchases chemical X from a local supplier at a cost of $3.00 per litre.It uses the economic order quantity(EOQ)model to determine order size and carries a safety inventory of five days'usage to protect against variations in lead time demand. Purdy Co has recently been approached by a company offering to supply chemical Xat a cost of $2.25 per litre,if it will order in quantities of 1 million litres.The new supplier's credit terms are the same as the current supplier.Purdy Co estimates that its storage cost per litre and its quality check cost per order will be unchanged if it accepts this offer and that it will have sufficient storage space to cope with larger orders.However,it will need to increase its safety inventory to 15 days'usage if it accepts the new supplier's offer. Purdy Co is under pressure from its bank to reduce its overdraft and its finance director is interested in the effect of the new supplier's offer on chemical X costs,working capital investment and the cash operating cycle. Purdy Co operates for 365 days per year.
